operating_state: Total revenue reached $9.6 billion in Q2 2024, up 17% year-over-year, driven by 16% growth in average paying memberships to 277.7 million and successful pricing optimization across regions. Global paid net membership additions reached 8.0 million, representing 37% growth versus prior year, with broad strength across all regions. Revenue per membership improved across all regions reflecting successful price increases and plan mix optimization, partially offset by unfavorable foreign exchange rates.
cost_structure: Operating margin expanded 500 basis points to 27% as revenue growth significantly outpaced expense increases. Cost of revenues declined as percentage of revenues to 54% from 57% prior year despite higher content amortization. Technology and development expenses rose driven by personnel costs and infrastructure investments, while marketing expenses increased modestly with higher personnel costs offset by lower advertising spend due to content release timing.
material_events: Netflix repurchased $1.6 billion of shares during Q2 and $3.6 billion year-to-date under the $15 billion authorization program, with $4.8 billion remaining available. The company established a new $3 billion revolving credit facility in April 2024, replacing the previous facility, and repaid $400 million of maturing Senior Notes. The 2024 annual meeting concluded with all nine directors elected and executive compensation approved.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency headwinds reduced revenues by approximately $430 million for the quarter and $701 million year-to-date compared to constant currency rates. The company expanded cash flow hedging programs to $9.5 billion notional amount to mitigate exchange rate volatility on forecasted foreign currency revenues and content obligations. Brazilian tax authorities continue assessments with potential exposure estimated at approximately $300 million.
forward_outlook: Content obligations increased to $23.3 billion from $21.7 billion at year-end, with management anticipating additional obligations over the next three years beyond contractual commitments. Production costs are paid upfront well before content availability and amortization. The company expects cash flows from operations, available funds, and financing sources including the new revolving credit facility will be sufficient to meet cash needs for the next twelve months and beyond.
uncertainty_profile: Content obligations include $16.9 billion not reflected on the balance sheet representing future titles with payment timing ranging from less than one year to more than five years. Brazilian non-income tax disputes remain unresolved with inherent complexity around tax treatment, while the company faces debt principal and interest payments due within twelve months and ongoing tax examinations with uncertain timing and outcomes.
